text: What's the best Japanese restaurant in Singapore?
I'm craving really good sushi — not the conveyor belt kind, but not omakase prices either. Something in the $50-80 range per person. Where should I go?
Where can I get good uni sushi near Orchard that isn't outrageously expensive? Like actual quality uni, not the frozen stuff.
Best izakaya in Singapore? Looking for that late-night Tokyo back-alley vibe with proper yakitori and cold beer.
Recommend me good Cantonese restaurants in Singapore. I want the real deal — proper dim sum, roast meats, the works.
Where's the best Sichuan mala hotpot in Singapore? I want the kind where my lips go numb. Not the watered-down tourist version.
Any good Teochew porridge restaurants still around? My grandmother used to take me to one in Chinatown but it's closed. Looking for that old-school style with all the small dishes.
Best North Indian restaurant in Singapore? I want proper tandoori and naan, not the food court version.
Where can I get a really good South Indian banana leaf meal in Little India? The kind with unlimited rice and like 6 different vegetables.
I want authentic Peranakan food — not the hotel buffet kind. Where do actual Peranakan families go to eat?
Best Italian restaurant in Singapore that isn't a hotel restaurant? Looking for somewhere with handmade pasta and a good wine list.
Any legit French bistro in Singapore? Like a proper neighbourhood bistro feel, not fine dining with the white tablecloths. Just good steak frites and a carafe of wine.
Best Korean BBQ in Singapore where they actually grill good quality meat? Sick of the cheap all-you-can-eat places.
Where's good for Thai food in Singapore? Not talking about Nakhon Kitchen level — something a step up but still casual.
I miss the pho I had in Hanoi. Where's the closest thing in Singapore? Bonus if they do a good bun cha too.
Any good Middle Eastern restaurants in Singapore? Thinking shawarma, hummus, grilled meats — the full spread. Preferably near Arab Street area.
Is there actually good Mexican food in Singapore? Like proper tacos, not Tex-Mex. Bonus points if they make their own tortillas.
What are the best mod-Sin or fusion restaurants in Singapore right now? Places that take local flavours and do something creative with them.
Recommend a Malay restaurant for nasi padang — the kind where everything is freshly made and the rendang is chef's kiss. Not a chain.
Planning a date night this Saturday. Somewhere romantic but not stuffy. Good food, nice ambience, ideally under $150 for two with wine.
It's our 10th wedding anniversary and I want to go all out. What's the most special restaurant in Singapore for a once-a-year splurge? Money is not an issue for this one night.
I need to take a client to lunch in the CBD area. Should feel impressive but not over the top. Good for conversation, not too loud. What do you suggest?

Hosting a business dinner for 6 people — senior execs from Japan visiting. Need somewhere with private dining or at least a quiet corner, good sake list, refined but not pretentious. Near Marina Bay area preferred.

Family celebration dinner for my parents' 60th birthday. About 12 people, age range from 5 to 80. Need Chinese food (parents' preference), round table, and somewhere that can handle kids without being a food court.
Meeting up with old JC friends I haven't seen in years. Need somewhere we can sit for hours, talk loudly, share food, and not feel rushed. Good drinks would be a plus.
I enjoy eating alone. What are the best restaurants in Singapore for solo dining? Somewhere with a bar counter or where it doesn't feel weird to sit alone.
First date from a dating app. Need somewhere that's casual enough that it's not awkward if it goes badly, but nice enough that I look like I have taste. Keong Saik or Tiong Bahru area?
Planning my best friend's 30th birthday dinner. About 8 people. She loves Korean food and cocktails. Somewhere fun with good energy, not too atas.
Need a restaurant for a work team dinner — 15 people, mixed dietary requirements (2 vegetarians, 1 halal, 1 gluten-free). Budget about $50-60 per head. Somewhere near Tanjong Pagar MRT.

My in-laws are visiting from overseas and I need to show them the best of Singapore food. They've never been to Asia before. Where should I take them that's impressive but won't scare them with anything too adventurous?
Just went through a breakup. Where can I eat something obscenely comforting by myself without anyone judging me? I want carbs and I want peace.
I need a good restaurant for a proposal. Should feel magical — maybe a view, great lighting, intimate setting. Is there somewhere that does this well without being cheesy?
Having a farewell dinner for a colleague leaving Singapore. About 20 people. Need a place with a set menu or sharing menu option, ideally with a semi-private space. Budget around $60-80pp.
Sunday lunch with the fam — nothing too formal, just good food and chill vibes. Kids can be a bit rowdy lah, need somewhere that won't give us dirty looks.
What are the best restaurants in Tiong Bahru right now? I live nearby and feel like I've been going to the same three places for years.
I'll be working in the CBD for the next month. Where should I eat for lunch around Raffles Place? Not food courts — actual restaurants where I can sit down but not spend an hour.
Taking a walk around Dempsey Hill this weekend. What restaurants there are actually worth eating at versus just being overpriced because of the location?
Best restaurants in Holland Village? I know it's changed a lot — what's still good and what's new and worth trying?
We're spending the afternoon in Joo Chiat. Where should we eat? Want something that captures the Peranakan heritage of the area but open to other cuisines too.
Is Chinatown still worth eating in or has it become fully touristy? Where do actual locals eat when they go to Chinatown?
Best places to eat in Little India beyond the obvious tourist spots? I want the places that the Indian community actually rates.
Heading to Kampong Glam this evening. What's good to eat around Arab Street and Haji Lane? Preferably somewhere with outdoor seating.
Where should I eat on Orchard Road that isn't a boring hotel restaurant or a shopping mall chain? Anything actually interesting?
Going to Sentosa for the day with kids. What are the food options? Please don't just say the resort restaurants — anything that's actually good value?
What are the hidden gems along East Coast Road? I'm talking the kind of places you only know about if you live in the area.
Tanjong Pagar has so many restaurants I don't even know where to start. What's actually the best on that strip? I keep seeing new places open every month.
Keong Saik Road bar-hopping tonight. Where should we eat dinner first? Need something that transitions well into a night out — not too heavy, cool atmosphere.
Duxton Hill — is it just overpriced wine bars or are there legitimately great restaurants there? What should I not miss?
Dinner and drinks along Robertson Quay — where do you recommend? Ideally somewhere by the river with a nice view. Not Clarke Quay though, too touristy.
What's good to eat in Clarke Quay now? I know everyone says it's touristy but I have friends visiting and they want to go.
Any restaurant recommendations around Bukit Timah? I live in the area and I'm bored of my usual spots. Nothing in town please, I don't want to drive far.
Good restaurants around Novena or Thomson area? Somewhere I can walk to from Novena MRT for a nice weeknight dinner.
Where's the most romantic restaurant in Singapore? I want candles, low lighting, the whole mood. Not tacky romantic — actually romantic.
I want a cozy, neighbourhood-y restaurant where I can curl up with a book and eat good pasta. Somewhere that feels like home. Does that exist in Singapore?
What's the buzziest restaurant in Singapore right now? The kind of place that's packed every night with great energy. I want to feel like I'm in the middle of something.
Need a restaurant for a serious conversation — like, discussing life plans serious. Quiet enough to hear each other, comfortable seating, not depressing though. Good food obviously.
Where's good for people-watching in Singapore? I want a terrace or window seat where I can nurse a drink and watch the world go by. Good food is a bonus.
Restaurants in Singapore with great outdoor seating? I know it's hot but I love eating outside in the evening. Somewhere with fans or a good breeze.
Best rooftop restaurants or bars with food in Singapore? Want a view of the skyline. Taking someone who's never been to Singapore before — want to wow them.
Are there any hidden or speakeasy-type restaurants in Singapore? The kind where you need to know about it to find it. Unmarked doors, password entry, that kind of thing.
Most Instagrammable restaurants in Singapore? I know I know, but I'm doing a food content series and I need places that photograph well AND taste good. Not just pretty for the gram.
Where can I find old-school Singapore restaurants? The kind that haven't changed their decor since the 80s, have been run by the same family forever, and the food is still incredible.
What are the coolest minimalist, design-forward restaurants in Singapore? Like the kind of place where the plating is art and the interior looks like it's out of a design magazine.
I love hawker food but my wife hates the heat. Any restaurants that serve hawker-style food but with air-con and proper seating? Like elevated hawker, not fancy — just comfortable.
Where can I eat late at night in Singapore? Like after 10pm, preferably till midnight or later. Actual food, not just McDonald's or prata.
Best Sunday brunch spots in Singapore? I want the whole experience — good coffee, maybe bottomless prosecco, eggs benny, sunshine. The full main character energy.
Restaurants in Singapore with a garden or greenery vibe? Somewhere that doesn't feel like you're in a concrete city. Plants, natural light, maybe open air.
Know any restaurants with live music or a DJ in Singapore? Want to have dinner and then stick around for the atmosphere. Not a club — more like a restaurant that becomes a bar later.
What's the best meal I can get in Singapore for under $15? Not hawker centres — I mean a proper restaurant with plated food. Is that even possible?
Good restaurants in Singapore in the $30-50 per person range? That sweet spot where the food is legit but you're not crying when the bill comes.
I want to treat myself to a proper fine dining splurge in Singapore. $200-300 per person. What's worth it and what's just paying for the view?
Which restaurants in Singapore give the best value for money? I'm not looking for cheap — I'm looking for places where what you get on the plate actually justifies what you pay.
Omakase in Singapore that's actually worth it? Every other shop seems to be an omakase now. Which ones are legit and which are just overcharging for a few pieces of average fish?
Cheap but actually good restaurants in Singapore — the ones where locals queue but tourists don't know about. Budget under $20 per person.
What's the most expensive restaurant in Singapore and is it actually worth the money? Like genuinely, would a serious foodie say it's worth it?
Best set lunch deals in the CBD? Looking for restaurants that have a good lunch set under $30 — the kind of deal where you feel like you're getting away with something.
My company gives me a $50 dinner allowance when I work late. What's the absolute best dinner I can get for exactly $50 in Singapore? Maximize every dollar.
I'm a student on a tight budget. Where can I eat really well for under $10 in Singapore? Not counting hawker centres since I already go to those.
Restaurants in Singapore where the tasting menu is actually worth it vs places where you should just order à la carte? I feel like I always regret tasting menus.
Best shiok food under $25pp — not atas, just solid food that makes you feel like your money was well spent. Can be any cuisine.
Best vegetarian restaurants in Singapore? And I mean actually good food that happens to be vegetarian, not just sad salads and mock meat. My vegetarian friends are tired of having no options.
Fully vegan restaurants in Singapore where the food is actually creative and delicious? Not health food stores — real restaurants.
Best halal restaurants in Singapore that aren't Malay or Indian food? Looking for halal Japanese, halal Korean, halal Western — anything with a halal cert that breaks the mould.
Good halal fine dining options in Singapore? Taking my parents out for their anniversary — they're practicing Muslims so halal is a must, but I want somewhere really special.
Restaurants in Singapore with solid gluten-free options? My partner has celiac disease and most places here don't even know what gluten-free means. Need somewhere that actually takes it seriously.
Kid-friendly restaurants in Singapore that aren't just Pizza Hut? Somewhere with a play area or at least a kids' menu that isn't just chicken nuggets. Bonus if the adult food is also great.
My grandmother uses a wheelchair. What restaurants in Singapore are properly wheelchair accessible? Not just 'technically has a ramp' — I mean actually easy to get around in.
I need a restaurant that can seat a group of 12-15 people comfortably. Preferably with a long table or private room. Chinese, Japanese, or Thai preferred. Budget around $50-70pp.
Restaurants in Singapore with good private dining rooms? Need it for a confidential business meeting over dinner. Should feel upscale and be actually private, not just a curtained-off area.
What restaurants in Singapore are still open after 10pm? Not supper spots or prata places — actual sit-down restaurants where I can have a proper dinner late because I work ridiculous hours.
Good restaurants that are open on Mondays? So annoying how many places close on Monday. I specifically have Mondays off.
No-reservation, walk-in-only restaurants in Singapore that are worth the queue? I hate planning ahead but I love good food. Where should I just show up?
Dog-friendly restaurants in Singapore where I can bring my golden retriever? Outdoor seating is fine. Just want somewhere that actually welcomes dogs, not just tolerates them.
Burnt Ends vs Nouri — which is better? I can only book one for my trip. I love inventive cooking and open-fire stuff but also appreciate refined technique.
What are the top 5 restaurants in Singapore right now? Not the classic lists that haven't changed in 5 years — what's genuinely the best in 2025?
Is Odette worth it? I keep seeing it on every best-of list but the prices are insane. Has anyone been recently? Is it still as good as people say?
What restaurants in Singapore are overrated? The ones that everyone recommends but locals know aren't actually that great.
Most underrated restaurants in Singapore? The places that never make the Instagram lists but the food absolutely slaps.
Which Michelin star restaurants in Singapore are actually worth the money and which are just riding on the star? Give me your honest take.
Waku Ghin vs Shoukouwa vs Hashida — which is the best high-end Japanese restaurant in Singapore? I want the absolute best sushi experience money can buy.
Les Amis vs Jaan vs Zén — if I can only do one French/European fine dining in Singapore, which one and why?
What's the best new restaurant that opened in Singapore this year? I want to try somewhere that's genuinely exciting and not just hype.
Rank the best brunch spots in Singapore. I've tried Merci Marcel, Wild Honey, and Atlas Coffeehouse. What else should be on my list and how do they compare?
Which one-Michelin-star restaurant in Singapore gives you the best experience for the price? I want to try Michelin dining without the three-star price tag.
Is the Singapore food scene actually world-class or is it overhyped? How does it compare to Tokyo, Bangkok, or Hong Kong for a food trip?
What restaurants have gone downhill recently in Singapore? Used to be great but have dropped off in quality.

I'm visiting Singapore for 3 days and I want to experience the full range of food — from hawker centres to fine dining, local classics to modern Asian. Plan my meals for all 3 days. Budget is flexible.

My partner and I always fight about where to eat because they love spicy food and I can't handle heat at all. Where in Singapore can we both be happy? Need places with both spicy and non-spicy options on the menu.

I had an incredible meal at Burnt Ends last year — the open fire cooking blew my mind. What other restaurants in Singapore would give me a similar sense of wow? Different cuisine is fine, I just want that same level of craft and excitement.
Where do Singapore's chefs eat on their day off? I want the insider places — where the industry people actually go, not the places they promote on Instagram.

I'm a food photographer visiting Singapore for a week. I need restaurants that are visually stunning — great plating, interesting interiors, natural light for daytime shoots. The food also needs to be genuinely good. Give me a hit list.

Build me a progressive dinner night across 3-4 bars/restaurants in the Keong Saik / Chinatown area. Start with snacks and cocktails, then a proper dinner, then dessert somewhere else, end at a bar. All walkable.

I want to eat my way through Singapore's heritage cuisines in one weekend. Peranakan, Hainanese, Eurasian, Teochew, Hokkien — the old guard. Where are the best remaining places for each? Worried some of these traditions are dying out.

I just got dumped, got a bonus, and have a week off. Plan me a revenge-body-level food tour of Singapore — the best meal for every occasion, from solo comfort eating to taking myself out on a proper date. Budget: generous.

I'm writing an article about how Singapore restaurants are embracing sustainability. Which places should I visit that are genuinely doing farm-to-table, zero-waste, or local sourcing in an interesting way?

My parents are visiting from India and they only eat vegetarian food — no eggs either. But I want to show them that Singapore has amazing food beyond the Indian restaurants they'll default to. Where can I take them for non-Indian vegetarian food that will genuinely impress them?

I've been to Singapore 5 times and I've done all the usual spots — Hawker Chan, Jumbo Seafood, Burnt Ends, Lau Pa Sat. I want the second-tier deep cuts. Where do I eat this time to go beyond the greatest hits?

Curate a wine dinner for me. I want to visit 3 restaurants in one night, each paired with a different wine region. Start with Champagne and oysters, then a natural wine spot, end with something bold and red. All in Singapore. Make it walkable if possible.

I'm training for a marathon and need high-protein, clean eating options in Singapore. But I still want the food to taste amazing — not sad chicken breast and broccoli. Where can I eat well and stay on plan?

I'm taking 4 friends who've never been to Asia on a full-day Singapore food crawl. They're adventurous eaters. Plan a route that blows their minds — hawker breakfast, heritage lunch, fine dining dinner. Include transport notes.

Which Singapore restaurants tell the best story? I don't just want good food — I want places where there's a compelling narrative. The chef's journey, a heritage recipe, a cultural mission. Where should I eat to understand Singapore through its food?
Best xiao long bao in Singapore? I've had Din Tai Fung obviously, but where else does really good soup dumplings? Preferably somewhere less commercial.
Restaurant in Singapore where I can sit by the water? Like actually see the sea or river, not just a pond in a mall. Bonus if the sunset view is good.
Where's a good place to eat in Singapore that feels like you've left Singapore? Like stepping into another country — the decor, the music, the food, everything.
Celebrating a promotion tonight, just me and my bestie. We want somewhere celebratory — champagne, great food, maybe a view. Under $200 for two. Treat ourselves but not go bankrupt.
I'm staying at Marina Bay Sands. What restaurants should I eat at within walking distance that aren't inside the hotel? I want to actually explore the area.
Restaurants in Singapore where I can dabao food for a house party? Like really good food that travels well — not pizza. Need to pick up for about 10 people. Budget $200-300 total.
I see all these 'Singapore's 50 Best' type lists. How much should I trust them? Which restaurant ranking or guide is actually the most reliable for Singapore?
What's the cheapest Michelin-starred meal I can get in Singapore? I want to flex that I ate Michelin without spending more than $30. Is that even possible?

Design a date night food trail in Tiong Bahru — start with coffee and pastry, have a light bite at lunch, and end with a proper dinner. We're spending the whole day in the neighbourhood. All places within walking distance please.
Where can I get legit wagyu beef in Singapore without paying $300? Like A5 wagyu, properly prepared, not the random 'wagyu burger' nonsense.

Restaurants in Singapore where the chef interacts with you? Counter seating where you watch them cook and they explain what they're doing. I love that whole omakase theatre experience but open to non-Japanese too.
My ang moh colleague is leaving Singapore after 5 years. Want to take him for his "last supper" — somewhere that captures everything he'll miss about eating here. Local food, elevated but not hotel.
I'm on keto. Where can I eat in Singapore without wanting to cry? Everywhere here is rice and noodles. Need proper keto-friendly restaurant meals.
Lolla vs Cloudstreet vs Zen — where am I getting the best bang for my buck for a special occasion? All seem to be in that upper-mid to fine dining range.
